tldr: Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser leave the \(\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\) terminal line of \(\mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}\) without a nonidentification certificate. This core defines a graph-only context-specific hedge together with a genuine finite binary context-selected SCM: the selector is exogenous, selector labels have serious assignment values, and only nonselector variables obey recursive response equations. The context-marginal sufficiency theorem asks whether the explicit parity response construction makes every supplied context law coupling-free while a post-intervention exposure condition makes the empty-context target coupling-sensitive. The headline open question is whether every \(\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\) instance contains that graph object, which would complete the published frontier.

symbols.name: \(X_v\)
symbols.type: Bernoulli random variable indexed by \(v\in V^\circ\)
symbols.def: Binary endogenous response at nonselector vertex \(v\).

symbols.name: \(z_\ell\)
symbols.def: Serious assignment value encoded by label \(\ell\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal U\)
symbols.def: Selector states whose conditional laws are supplied.

symbols.name: \(\rho_u(H)\)
symbols.def: The edge restriction of \(H\) active at selector state \(u\).

symbols.name: \(a\)
symbols.def: Treatment value in the target intervention.

symbols.name: \(y^\star\)
symbols.def: Outcome vertex witnessing post-intervention two-block exposure when that predicate holds.

symbols.name: \(R\)
symbols.def: Common roots of the forest witness.

symbols.name: \(F_0,F_L,F_R,F_1\)
symbols.def: Respectively the common, left, right, and union forests.

symbols.name: \(T_L,T_R\)
symbols.def: The disjoint left and right parity-block vertex sets.

symbols.name: \(\operatorname{Conn}_u(B,R;H)\)
symbols.def: Predicate that \(\rho_u(H)\) has a directed path from \(B\) to \(R\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal H_{\mathrm{CS}}\)
symbols.def: The set of context-specific hedges for \((\bar G,A,Y,\mathcal U)\).

symbols.name: \(U_e\)
symbols.type: Bernoulli random variable indexed by \(e\in E^{\leftrightarrow}\)
symbols.def: Latent response coordinate shared by the endpoints of \(e\).

symbols.name: \(M_\kappa\)
symbols.def: Explicit selector-aware parity response construction indexed by \(\kappa\).

symbols.name: \(c_{y^\star}\)
symbols.def: The nonzero Fourier coefficient of the nonparity response noise in \(X_{y^\star}\) under \(\operatorname{do}(A=a,S=\emptyset)\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal I\)
symbols.def: A label-set graph, available contexts, intervention, and target supplied to \(\mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}\).

symbols.name: \(\mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}\)
symbols.def: The systematic-selection identification algorithm of Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser.

definitions.name: Serious selector assignment
definitions.construction: \(I_v(u)=\mathbf 1\{\exists\ell\in u:W_\ell=v\}\) and, when \(I_v(u)=1\), \(z_v(u)=z_\ell\) for any \(\ell\in u\) with \(W_\ell=v\).

definitions.name: Rooted \(C\)-forest predicate
definitions.construction: \(\operatorname{CF}_R(F)\Longleftrightarrow F^{\leftrightarrow}\text{ is connected},\ \forall v\in\operatorname{v}(F)\setminus R\ \exists!w\in\operatorname{v}(F):(v\to w)\in F,\ \text{and}\ \forall r\in R\ \neg\exists w\in\operatorname{v}(F):(r\to w)\in F.\)

definitions.name: Context connectivity
definitions.construction: \(\operatorname{Conn}_u(B,R;H)\Longleftrightarrow\exists b\in B,\exists r\in R,\exists(v_0,\ldots,v_m):v_0=b,\ v_m=r,\ \bigwedge_{j=0}^{m-1}(v_j\to v_{j+1})\in\rho_u(H).\)

definitions.name: Post-intervention two-block exposure
definitions.construction: \(\operatorname{Exp}_a(T_L,T_R;Y;F_1)\Longleftrightarrow\exists y^\star\in Y,\exists t_L\in T_L,\exists t_R\in T_R\ \text{such that}\ \rho_{\emptyset}(\bar G)[V^\circ\setminus A]\ \text{contains exactly one directed path}\ t_L\rightsquigarrow y^\star\ \text{and exactly one directed path}\ t_R\rightsquigarrow y^\star,\ \text{both contained in}\ F_1,\ \text{and neither path has an internal vertex in}\ A\cup\{S\}.\)

definitions.name: Target contrast coefficient
definitions.construction: \(c_{y^\star}=\mathbb E\!\left[(-1)^{X_{y^\star}+B_L+B_R}\mid\operatorname{do}(A=a,S=\emptyset)\right]\), where the expectation is evaluated under \(\kappa=0\).

definitions.name: Context-specific hedge
definitions.construction:
\(\mathcal H_{\mathrm{CS}}( ar G,A,Y,\mathcal U)=\{\mathfrak H=(F_0,F_L,F_R,F_1,R,T_L,T_R):\operatorname{CF}_R(F_0)\wedge\operatorname{CF}_R(F_L)\wedge\operatorname{CF}_R(F_R)\wedge F_0\subsetneq F_L\subseteq F_1\wedge F_0\subsetneq F_R\subseteq F_1\wedge T_L=\operatorname{v}(F_L)\setminus\operatorname{v}(F_0)\wedge T_R=\operatorname{v}(F_R)\setminus\operatorname{v}(F_0)\wedge T_L\cap T_R=\emptyset\wedge\operatorname{v}(F_1)=\operatorname{v}(F_0)\cup T_L\cup T_R\wedge\operatorname{v}(F_0)\cap A=\emptyset\wedge\operatorname{v}(F_1)\cap A
e\emptyset\wedge R\subseteq\operatorname{An}_{ ar G[V^\circ\setminus A]}(Y)\wedge\operatorname{Exp}_a(T_L,T_R;Y;F_1)\wedge orall u\in\mathcal U\,[
eg\operatorname{Conn}_u(T_L,R;F_L)\vee
eg\operatorname{Conn}_u(T_R,R;F_R)]\wedge\operatorname{Conn}_{\emptyset}(T_L,R;F_L)\wedge\operatorname{Conn}_{\emptyset}(T_R,R;F_R)\}.

definitions.name: Selector-aware parity CS-SCM
definitions.construction: \(M_\kappa=\operatorname{CSParity}(\bar G,\mathfrak H,\pi,\kappa):\ S=Z_S\sim\pi;\ \Pr_\kappa(B_L=i,B_R=j)=\tfrac14[1+\kappa(-1)^{i+j}];\ \Pr(U_e=1)=\tfrac14;\ (B_L,B_R),\{U_e:e\in E^{\leftrightarrow}\},Z_S\ \text{are mutually independent};\ \text{in a topological order of}\ (V^\circ,E^{\to}),\ X_v=I_v(S)z_v(S)\oplus[1-I_v(S)]\bigl(\bigoplus_{(w\to v)\in E^{\to}}\mathbf 1\{\lambda(w\to v)\cap S=\emptyset\}X_w\oplus\bigoplus_{e\in E^{\leftrightarrow}:v\in e}\mathbf 1\{\lambda(e)\cap S=\emptyset\}U_e\oplus\mathbf 1\{v\in T_L\}B_L\oplus\mathbf 1\{v\in T_R\}B_R\bigr),\ \text{where}\ \oplus\ \text{is addition modulo}\ 2.\)

definitions.name: Uncovered SS-ID failure class
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal C_{\mathrm{cs2}}=\{\mathcal I:\mathcal I\ \text{is a finite SS-ID input and}\ \mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}(\mathcal I)=\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\}.\)

sampling_model.design: A finite CS-SCM supplies the population laws \(P_{u,\kappa}=p_{M_\kappa}(V^\circ\mid S=u)\) for \(u\in\mathcal U\).
sampling_model.units: One population CS-SCM has selector \(S\in2^{\mathcal L}\) and binary nonselector vertices.
sampling_model.overlap: Each supplied selector context has positive probability under \(\pi\).
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: Not applicable: the proposal concerns population-level graphical identification.

statements.statement: For every \(\mathfrak H\in\mathcal H_{\mathrm{CS}}(\bar G,A,Y,\mathcal U)\) and \(\kappa\in(-1,1)\), \(M_\kappa\) is a well-defined finite binary CS-SCM: \(S\) is generated only by \(Z_S\), every serious selector assignment overrides the corresponding nonselector response equation, and conditioning on \(S=u\) activates exactly the edge copies of \(\rho_u(\bar G)\).
statements.justification: The countermodel must be a valid CS-SCM before equality of its context laws has any identification implication.
statements.gap: The ordinary parity hedge has no selector equation or serious-assignment override, and the previous draft assigned \(S\) twice.
statements.consumer: It licenses use of the constructed pair as admissible systematic-selection models.

statements.statement: For every \(\mathfrak H\in\mathcal H_{\mathrm{CS}}(\bar G,A,Y,\mathcal U)\) and all \(\kappa,\kappa'\in(-1,1)\), \(P_{u,\kappa}=P_{u,\kappa'}\) for every \(u\in\mathcal U\). If \(y^\star\) witnesses \(\operatorname{Exp}_a(T_L,T_R;Y;F_1)\), then \(c_{y^\star}>0\) and \(\mathbb E_{Q_\kappa}[(-1)^{X_{y^\star}}]=c_{y^\star}\kappa\); hence \(Q_{1/2}\ne Q_{-1/2}\). In particular, \(Q\) is not identifiable from \(\{P_{u,\kappa}:u\in\mathcal U\}\) over finite binary CS-SCMs compatible with \(\bar G\).
statements.justification: Context severing must erase the coupled parity coordinate from every supplied full law, while the explicit post-intervention contrast makes target separation checkable.
statements.gap: Shpitser--Pearl prove correlation hiding for an ordinary hedge, and Lee--Correa--Bareinboim synchronize supplied experiments; neither result handles label-induced restrictions of one CS-SCM or the intervention-safe exposure condition.
statements.consumer: A detected CS-hedge becomes a concrete nonidentification certificate for systematic-selection data fusion.

statements.statement: If the supplied-law collection is augmented by \(P_{\emptyset,\kappa}=p_{M_\kappa}(V^\circ\mid S=\emptyset)\) and both blocks are required to be severed in the observational empty context, then \(P_{\emptyset,1/2}=P_{\emptyset,-1/2}\) and the pair \((F_0,F_1)\) in \(\mathfrak H\) is the ordinary rooted-forest parity witness for nonidentification of \(p(Y\mid\operatorname{do}(A=a))\).
statements.justification: The reduction now includes the observed law that the ordinary hedge theorem actually requires.
statements.gap: This is a sanity reduction to Shpitser--Pearl rather than a claimed improvement over that result.
statements.consumer: It checks that the selector-aware construction collapses to the known one-law witness under an explicitly supplied empty context.

statements.statement: For every \(\mathcal I\in\mathcal C_{\mathrm{cs2}}\), does the label-set ADMG produced at the terminal \(\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\) call contain an element of \(\mathcal H_{\mathrm{CS}}(\bar G,A,Y,\mathcal U)\)?
statements.justification: This is the unresolved graphical extraction step, not a secondary necessity question.
statements.gap: Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser give nonidentification certificates for their other terminal cases but leave \(\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\) without one.
statements.consumer: An affirmative answer supplies a countermodel pair at every remaining terminal failure.

statements.statement: If Open-ended Question 1 has an affirmative answer, then for every finite binary CS-SCM input \(\mathcal I\), \(\mathsf{SS\text{-}ID}(\mathcal I)\) succeeds if and only if \(p(Y\mid\operatorname{do}(A=a,S=\emptyset))\) is identifiable from its supplied selector-context laws.
statements.justification: Published soundness plus the existing failure certificates leave precisely the \(\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\) converse to close.
statements.gap: Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser prove soundness and conjecture completeness; this is the exact comparator-facing frontier conditional on the new extraction result.
statements.consumer: It turns SS-ID into a complete graphical decision procedure on the stated finite binary domain.

project_justification.gap: The systematic-selection literature lacks a graph certificate that is simultaneously invisible in every supplied selector context and visible after the target intervention.
project_justification.niche: The relevant obstruction is not an ordinary hedge alone: it must combine selector-assignment semantics, contextwise severing, and a path condition that survives \(\operatorname{do}(A=a)\).
project_justification.fill: The core exhibits a selector-aware finite parity CS-SCM, defines the CS-hedge that indexes it, and isolates extraction from the exact terminal \(\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\) graph as the first-class open problem.

related_work: Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser define CS-SCMs and prove SS-ID sound while leaving \(\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\) outside their nonidentification analysis. Shpitser and Pearl provide the rooted-forest hedge and parity response-function witness recovered by the explicit empty-context reduction. Lee, Correa, and Bareinboim develop hedgelets and thickets for multiple supplied experiments, but those laws are not label-induced restrictions of one structural system. Kivva, Mokhtarian, Etesami, and Kiyavash show that positivity can alter gID completeness, which motivates keeping the supplied selector contexts positive. Bareinboim and Pearl characterize transportability through selection diagrams, not selection into experimental contexts with serious assignments.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: The catalogue predecessor \(\texttt{eid\_data\_fusion\_k\_source}\) proposed a global overlap-hedge converse without a context-uniform construction. This core instead exhibits a local CS-hedge and an intervention-safe target-exposure condition, then makes the exact graph-extraction problem explicit.
novelty_justification.published_axis: The new object is the conjunction of selector-aware parity semantics, uniform context severing, and post-intervention two-block exposure. LeeGhassamiShpitser2024Systematic identifies the missing terminal branch; ShpitserPearl2006Joint and LeeCorreaBareinboim2020General provide the two separate witness ancestors, not their label-restricted synthesis.

honest_scope: The displayed parity construction is finite and binary. The context-marginal theorem and extraction question remain to be proved. The core does not claim arbitrary-alphabet completeness, positivity-genericity, or an unconditional SS-ID completeness theorem before Open-ended Question 1 is resolved.

bibliography.citation: J. J. R. Lee, A. Ghassami, and I. Shpitser. A General Identification Algorithm for Data Fusion Problems under Systematic Selection. UAI, 2024. arXiv:2404.06602.
I. Shpitser and J. Pearl. Identification of Joint Interventional Distributions in Recursive Semi-Markovian Causal Models. AAAI, 2006.
S. Lee, J. D. Correa, and E. Bareinboim. General Identifiability with Arbitrary Surrogate Experiments. UAI, 2020, PMLR 115:389--398.
Y. Kivva, E. Mokhtarian, J. Etesami, and N. Kiyavash. Revisiting the General Identifiability Problem. UAI, 2022. arXiv:2206.01081.
E. Bareinboim and J. Pearl. Transportability of Causal Effects: Completeness Results. AAAI, 2012.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: SS-ID is sound, and the \(\mathtt{cs\_fail\_2}\) terminal line is left without a nonidentification certificate.
An ordinary rooted-forest hedge has a binary parity nonidentification witness.
Hedgelets and thickets synchronize nonidentification witnesses across arbitrary supplied experiments.
Positivity changes the general-identifiability completeness problem.
Selection-diagram transportability has a complete graphical characterization.

literature_checklist.author: Lee, Ghassami, and Shpitser
literature_checklist.one_line: Defines CS-SCMs and SS-ID, proves soundness, and leaves the confounded-selector failure without a converse certificate.

literature_checklist.author: Shpitser and Pearl
literature_checklist.one_line: Provides rooted-forest hedges and parity response-function witnesses for ordinary interventional nonidentification.

literature_checklist.author: Lee, Correa, and Bareinboim
literature_checklist.one_line: Provides hedgelets and thickets for preserving several supplied experimental laws.

literature_checklist.author: Kivva et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Shows that positivity is consequential for general-identifiability completeness and witness constructions.

literature_checklist.author: Bareinboim and Pearl
literature_checklist.one_line: Establishes a graphical completeness result for transportability under selection diagrams.
